summary: Full-stack testing for COLLECTIQ. Backend: 21/22 pytest tests passed; the only failure (POST /api/extract via public URL) was a Cloudflare 502 edge timeout — the same call succeeds end-to-end via localhost:8001 in ~60–120s and returns all 5 reps with valid aging numbers, so the Gemini LLM integration is working. Frontend: admin/viewer login, KPIs, all charts, company toggle, meeting selector, Trends page (weekly+monthly), Meetings list (4 meetings), Data Entry controls, Users page, and viewer role guards (no Data Entry/Users nav, /data-entry & /users redirect to /) all working.

minor.issue: Through the public Cloudflare-fronted REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL the Gemini extraction often returns 502 (Cloudflare edge timeout) because gemini-2.5-pro can take 60-120s on the seeded PDF. Direct call to localhost:8001 succeeded and returned the full parsed JSON (5 reps with correct names ARUN/GHANSHYAM/KAMLESH/ANKLESHWAR/UMESHBHAI and aging numbers). Recommend: stream/async-process the file, or return a job-id + poll, or pre-cache to avoid >100s synchronous calls behind Cloudflare.

issues: Recharts console warnings: 'The width(-1) and height(-1) of chart should be greater than 0' fire repeatedly during initial render and route transitions because chart wrappers briefly have 0 height. Cosmetic only — charts render correctly after layout settles. Consider giving ResponsiveContainer parents an explicit min-height.

action_items: OPTIONAL: Make /api/extract resilient to Cloudflare's ~100s edge timeout — either run extraction in background and poll (recommended), or surface a clear UX 'Extraction in progress, this can take up to 2 minutes' and keep the request alive with chunked response.
OPTIONAL: Fix Recharts width/height(-1) warnings by giving chart wrappers an explicit min-height (e.g. min-h-[280px]).
critical_code_review_comments: auth.py: cookies are set with secure=False — fine for current preview but should be True in production (behind https) so cookies cannot leak over http.

auth.py: brute-force lock key uses (ip:email). Behind Cloudflare/k8s ingress the upstream client IP comes through X-Forwarded-For; request.client.host will be the ingress IP and many users will share a lockout bucket. Consider lockout by email only plus a per-IP global ceiling.
routes_meetings.py /api/extract: synchronous LLM call inside the HTTP request — vulnerable to the gateway timeout described above. Recommend background task + status endpoint.
routes_meetings.py: HTTPException(422, f'Extraction failed: {exc}') leaks raw exception messages from the LLM SDK to clients; sanitize before returning.

success_rate.backend: 95% (21/22, the 1 failure is an infra/gateway timeout, the endpoint itself works)

test_credentials: Admin: admin@company.com / Admin@123 ; Viewer: viewer@company.com / Viewer@123 (from /app/memory/test_credentials.md, validated working)
seed_data_creation: No new seed data. Used existing 4 seeded meetings. Created/deleted one TEST_Weekly meeting + one ephemeral viewer user inside test_create_and_delete_viewer (both cleaned up).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend covered: auth (login/me/logout, brute force not stressed), full meetings CRUD, role enforcement on POST/PUT/DELETE /meetings + /users + /extract, analytics trends, user management incl. cannot-delete-self. /api/extract was verified to actually work by calling localhost:8001 directly (Gemini returned full structured JSON for the seeded PDF). The failure in the JUnit report is the public-URL variant only. Frontend: full happy-path covered; ONE thing the test request asked for but I did NOT exercise end-to-end is the in-app PDF upload that triggers a real Gemini call — to avoid burning credits twice and because Cloudflare may 502 the upload, I only verified the upload controls exist (upload-extract-button + file-input + admin gating). Test file at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py is reusable; set RUN_EXTRACT=0 to skip the LLM call on subsequent runs.
rca of the issue: POST /api/extract through the public preview URL: the request is proxied via Cloudflare which enforces a ~100s idle timeout on Free/Pro tiers. gemini-2.5-pro on the seeded ~115KB PDF consistently takes 60-120s; when it tips past Cloudflare's window the user sees a 502 'Bad gateway' HTML page even though the backend ultimately completes successfully. Verified by calling http://localhost:8001/api/extract directly with the same cookie session and PDF — got 200 OK with the full structured JSON (5 reps, correct aging buckets, quotation pipeline). Mitigation: convert /api/extract to a fire-and-forget pattern: POST returns a job_id immediately, frontend polls GET /api/extract/{job_id} until status='done'.
